name: White Chocolate Roulage
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly oil the bottom and sides of a 15" x 10" x 1-inch jellyroll pan with vegetable oil. Line with wax paper and lightly oil and flour the wax paper. Set aside.
2. In an electric mixer, beat egg yolks until foamy. Gradually add 1/4 cup sugar, beating until thick and lemon-colored (about 5 minutes). Stir in vegetable oil and vanilla extract. Set aside.
3. Beat egg whites to a foam at room temperature. Gradually add the remaining 1/2 cup sugar, beating until stiff but not dry.
4. Fold the white of egg mixture into the yolk mixture.
5. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually fold into the egg mixture.
6. Spread batter evenly into the prepared pan.
7. Bake at 350°F for 8 to 10 minutes.
8. Sift powdered sugar over a cloth towel in a 15" x 10-inch rectangle.
9. When the cake is done, loosen from the sides of the pan and turn out onto the sugared towel. Carefully peel off the wax paper.
10. Starting at the narrow end, roll up the cake and towel together. Let cool completely on a wire rack, seam side down.
11. Unroll the cake. Spread half of the White Chocolate Cream Filling over the top and carefully reroll the cake without the towel. Place on a serving plate with the seam side down. Spread remaining filling over all sides. Garnish with white chocolate curls.
